fields.result_summary: `grove-39d` is finished. UBS panic/assert noise is reduced on the practical repo workflow by ignoring dedicated Rust integration tests in project-scoped UBS runs, updating the repo guidance to use `ubs --diff .` / `ubs --staged .`, and removing `unwrap`/`expect` from the inline source-file test modules that still remain inside active Rust scan paths.
warnings: `ubs --only=rust --diff .` and `ubs --only=rust --staged .` both exit `0`, but still report `124` warnings on the changed Rust surface: `122` are inline `assert!` inventory, plus `1` `unreachable!` warning and `1` `thread::sleep in async` warning.

I did not create a commit or push. The shared worktree currently has staged `.grove` artifacts and git index writes are failing with `fatal: ... .git/index.lock: Read-only file system`, so cleanup/commit was not possible in this run.
Current verification passed: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets --no-run`.

lessons: Child `tests.rs` modules are a low-risk extraction pattern for Rust unit tests that need parent-module private access.
Narrow UBS ignore patterns for extracted test-only source files reduce assert noise without hiding production code.
